title: saxifraga adscendens var. aquatica
intro: Saxifraga adscendens var. aquatica, also known as the water saxifrage or aquatic saxifrage, is a variant of the saxifraga adscendens plant that is native to mountainous regions in Europe. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine, where it is valued for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues and soothe irritated skin. The health benefits of saxifraga adscendens var. aquatica include its ability to ease coughs, reduce fever, and provide relief from gastrointestinal problems, such as diarrhea and digestive issues. Saxifraga adscendens var. aquatica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its leaves and roots can also be used to make a poultice or salve for topical application.
